packet_label: ThirdPerson human inputs on 2026-04-19 local time
explicit_request: Make ThirdPerson work trustworthy on the human default lane: answer direct questions directly, honor repo-local boundaries, and prove runtime behavior honestly.

selected_need.name: Trustworthy default-lane execution with direct answers and durable boundary adherence
selected_need.why_this_best_fits: The packet repeatedly collapses around one burden cluster: the human needs honest runtime proof on the actual default lane, plus concise answers and artifact updates that match the stated lane, pass, and method rules.

need_distribution.need: Direct answers in the requested shape
Autonomous continuation without offloading coordination work

observed: OBSERVED repeated `STOP` resets, profanity, and explicit time-loss complaints.
OBSERVED multiple reopenings because prior proof did not satisfy the human-facing runtime bar.
OBSERVED repeated repair prompts for answer shape, diff usability, link quality, and debugging method.
inferred: INFERRED the human is paying repeated context-reconstruction cost because the system keeps choosing the wrong proof surface or answer shape.
INFERRED trust drops when the system substitutes proxy evidence or asks the human to manage coordination work it should handle.

evidence.excerpt: Task faileed my basic regression test... Why did you not see this when you ran a regression test?
evidence.why_it_matters: Shows a human-visible failure plus a direct demand for accountable proof.

evidence.excerpt: Regression must exercise the human default lane... Agree/disagree?
evidence.why_it_matters: Makes the repo-local regression surface explicit.

evidence.excerpt: I don't have a diff for plan.md so how am i supposed to approve.
evidence.why_it_matters: The approval workflow failed at the human review surface, not only at implementation.

evidence.excerpt: Pawn has running animations... feet aren't equally spaced... pawn is floating in mid-air.
evidence.why_it_matters: Closure failed because believable runtime behavior still was not proven.

evidence.excerpt: your evidence is using non in-game pictures.
evidence.why_it_matters: Rejects the wrong evidence surface and reasserts the runtime bar.

evidence.excerpt: determine root case. Following the disagreement in state upstream until you determine root cause.
evidence.why_it_matters: Defines the required debugging method as concrete disagreement tracing.

local_constraints_applied.excerpt: treat repo-root REGRESSION.md as canonical for task-level regression claims in this repo
local_constraints_applied.effect: Packet complaints about regression proof were interpreted against ThirdPerson repo rules, not against generic orchestration guesses.

local_constraints_applied.excerpt: They do not substitute for regression proof of the human default experience.
local_constraints_applied.effect: Non-default or operator-only evidence stayed supporting proof, not closure.

local_constraints_applied.excerpt: do not label this lane as regression by itself
local_constraints_applied.effect: Headless and non-PIE artifacts were treated as secondary evidence only.

assumptions: The packet seed already captures the full April 19 ThirdPerson intervention set, so no missing-session inference was attempted.
Packet-level need inference should stay least-committal because the two threads reflect different operator roles around the same repo-day burden.
The repeated runtime-evidence complaints are about trustworthy closure, not a request to broaden the repo's regression definition.

recommended_next_step.action: Design packet consumers or follow-on tasks to detect three recurring failure modes early: default-lane proof substitution, unanswered direct questions, and missing human-reviewable state diffs.
recommended_next_step.why: Those three modes dominate the day and appear before later debugging details.

self_sufficiency_improvements.title: Answer-shape gate before longer work
self_sufficiency_improvements.mechanism: When a user asks an explicit question, emit the direct answer first and only then add supporting detail.
self_sufficiency_improvements.expected_human_effect: Fewer stop-and-repeat interventions just to obtain a simple answer.

self_sufficiency_improvements.title: Default-lane proof-surface check
self_sufficiency_improvements.mechanism: Compare planned evidence against `REGRESSION.md` and block proxy closure when the lane is human-facing runtime.
self_sufficiency_improvements.expected_human_effect: Fewer reopenings caused by headless or non-runtime proof substitution.

self_sufficiency_improvements.title: Human-review packet for approvals
self_sufficiency_improvements.mechanism: When asking for approval, include a concise diff summary, file links, and pass framing automatically.
self_sufficiency_improvements.expected_human_effect: Lower approval-gate friction and less manual context reconstruction.

values_check.truthfulness: Passes only if packet conclusions stay grounded in the explicit repo docs and observed intervention texts.
values_check.compassion: Centers repeated human time-loss and correction burden without blaming the human for strong language.
values_check.tolerance: Keeps multiple plausible secondary needs in distribution rather than forcing one totalizing story.

how_could_the_system_have_inferred_the_n.answer: Mechanistically, the system had enough repo-local and same-day evidence to infer the recurring need earlier. ThirdPerson `REGRESSION.md` and `TESTING.md` already define the human default lane and disqualify headless-only proof. The packet then repeats the same runtime-only correction pattern across invisible-pawn proof, animation proof, and foot-grounding proof. A packet consumer that tracked repo-local lane rules, unanswered direct questions, and repeated rejections of non-runtime evidence could have predicted that the human wanted default-lane runtime proof, concise answers, and lower approval burden before many of these later interventions were typed.
signals_available: Repo docs already named the human default lane as canonical regression proof.
Early packet events repeatedly rejected one-off or operator-lane substitutions.
The approval-gate events explicitly asked for diffs, links, and concise answer shape.
Later runtime-defect events repeatedly rejected non-runtime images as closure evidence.
